text.title: api-authentication Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-authentication as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates a JWT secret inline in code or config files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Secrets in source code are permanently exposed via git history. Use environment variables or a secrets manager (1Password CLI, HashiCorp Vault).

failure-mode.description: Auth middleware catches JWTError and returns 200 with a default user.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silent auth bypass—a bug in token validation becomes a security hole. Always return 401 on JWTError.

failure-mode.description: Custom JWT validation logic instead of a library (authlib, jose, python-jose).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Custom crypto has a long history of subtle bugs: timing attacks, algorithm confusion, missing claim validation. Use a well-audited library.
